title: PhD
biography: Kizzmekia Corbett is an American immunologist and viral immunologist who was a key scientist behind the Moderna COVID-19 mRNA vaccine. She led the coronavirus team at the NIH Vaccine Research Center that designed the spike protein immunogen used in mRNA-1273, which has been administered to hundreds of millions worldwide.
aiSummary: Kizzmekia Corbett is an immunology specialist with an H-index of 40 at Harvard T.H. Chan School of Public Health. Key scientist behind the Moderna COVID-19 mRNA vaccine spike protein design. Based in Boston, United States.
techniquesInvented: Designed spike protein immunogen for mRNA-1273 (Moderna COVID vaccine)
education: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill

affiliations.hospitalName: Harvard T.H. Chan School of Public Health
affiliations.role: Assistant Professor of Immunology and Infectious Diseases
